title: Tetragonanthus elongatus
taxonomy: Tetragonanthus elongatus is classified as a species of plant in the family Fabaceae. This plant is a part of the genus Tetragonanthus. It is a flowering plant native to tropical Africa. The classification of Tetragonanthus elongatus is further broken down into the kingdom Plantae and the clade Angiosperms. The precise classification of Tetragonanthus elongatus is as follows: kingdom Plantae, clade Angiosperms, clade Eudicots, clade Rosids, order Fabales, family Fabaceae, genus Tetragonanthus, species T. elongatus.
uses: Tetragonanthus elongatus is native to Africa and has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. The plant has been used to treat conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. In some cultures, the plant is also used as an anti-inflammatory agent to reduce swelling and ease pain. However, more research is needed to confirm the effectiveness of these uses. Further studies are required to understand the full potential of Tetragonanthus elongatus in medicinal applications.

parts: Tetragonanthus elongatus, commonly known as the long-stalked calathea, is a plant species that has been explored for its medicinal properties. intro: Tetragonanthus elongatus, commonly known as the elongated tetragonanthus, is a plant species belonging to the family Apocynaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine due to its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, making it useful for treating various conditions such as wounds, fever, and respiratory issues. Bioactive constituents of Tetragonanthus elongatus include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, and is commonly used to treat fever, cough, and skin conditions.
